items.description: after the up() migration runs, the stock table has a batch_number column
after the up() migration runs, the stock table has a serial_number column
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12-B7-S001' has batch_number='B7' and serial_number='S001' after the backfill
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12-B7' has batch_number='B7' and serial_number=NULL after the backfill
a stock row with inventory_code 'A12' has batch_number=NULL and serial_number=NULL after the backfill
every row in a 100-row stock table has batch_number and serial_number present (populated or NULL) after the migration, with no rows skipped
the location column value is unchanged for a row after the migration runs
sku and quantity are unchanged for a row after the migration runs
the Alembic revision module does not import app/routes or app/services; persistence logic stays in the repository/migration layer
the app and Alembic env read DB_NAME/PGDATABASE from the environment; no hardcoded DSN exists anywhere in the migration or config path
after up() then down(), the original inventory_code value is reconstructed for every row and the batch_number/serial_number columns are removed
the UNIQUE(sku, location) constraint exists on the stock table after the migration and rejects a duplicate (sku, location) pair
quantity values are identical for every row before and after the migration; no negative quantity is introduced
the integration test suite connects to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow) and not an in-memory substitute; the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any scenario runs
the validation probe function can be called against the migrated stock table and returns a result without raising an error
when every stock row has a three-segment inventory_code the validation probe returns a count of 0
when the stock table contains rows with non-conforming codes (e.g. 'A12' and 'c') the probe returns a count equal to the number of rows where batch_number IS NULL OR serial_number IS NULL
a database administrator executing the validation query receives a scalar integer count that can be read and recorded before the drop proceeds
the validation probe module (app/repositories) does not import app/routes or app/services; the probe is never re-implemented outside the repository layer

the S2 validation test suite binds to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory substitute is used and the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any probe scenario runs
after the S3 up() migration runs, the stock table has no inventory_code column
after the S3 down() migration runs, the inventory_code column is present on the stock table
after the S3 down() migration runs, every row has a non-NULL inventory_code value
a row where batch_number is NULL has its inventory_code reconstructed with an empty string in the batch segment position (e.g., 'LOC--S001')
a row where serial_number is NULL has its inventory_code reconstructed with an empty string in the serial segment position (e.g., 'LOC-B7-')
after the S3 down() migration runs, the batch_number column is STILL PRESENT (preserved; owned by the S1 split migration, not retired by S3.down)
after the S3 down() migration runs, the serial_number column is STILL PRESENT (preserved; owned by the S1 split migration, not retired by S3.down)
after the S3 down() migration runs, the inventory_code column carries a NOT NULL constraint and an attempt to insert a NULL value is rejected
the S3 Alembic revision module does not import app/routes or app/services; the migration stays in the repository/migration layer and never crosses the layering contract

the S3 integration test suite binds to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory substitute is used and the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any S3 scenario runs
the home stock table renders a 'Batch Number' column and a 'Serial Number' column when stock records with populated batch_number and serial_number exist
the SKU detail view renders batch number and serial number as separate labeled fields for each location row when the stock record has batch_number and serial_number populated
a stock row with a NULL batch_number renders 'not tracked' in the Batch Number column of the home stock table
a stock row with a NULL serial_number renders 'not tracked' in the Serial Number column of the home stock table
a stock record with a NULL batch_number at a location renders 'not tracked' in the batch number field of the SKU detail view
a stock record with a NULL serial_number at a location renders 'not tracked' in the serial number field of the SKU detail view

the stock-list route (app/routes/) does not import app/repositories or the DB session; batch_number and serial_number are projected at the route boundary with no column computation inside the component

the S4 E2E test suite binds to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory substitute is used and the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any S4 scenario runs
